macro_summary: US equity futures are modestly softer (-0.2% S&P) as markets return from the Juneteenth holiday and absorb a hawkish post-FOMC hangover: Chair Warsh's first meeting ended with a unanimous hold at 3.5%-3.75% but nine of 18 dot-plot participants now project at least one rate hike by year-end, pushing the 2-year Treasury yield to a new cycle high above 4.22% Monday morning. Geopolitical risk remains binary — an interim US-Iran ceasefire has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and eased oil from its $113 peak to ~$78-80/bbl, but weekend talks in Bürgenstock were abruptly postponed and a final deal remains 60 days away at best. This week is data-rich and catalyst-dense: FedEx earnings Tuesday, Micron earnings and Fed bank stress tests Wednesday, and the critical May PCE inflation print (Fed's preferred measure) on Wednesday June 25 — all against the backdrop of a June 30 copper tariff deadline.
key_events: FDX (FedEx) Q4 FY2026 earnings after close Tuesday June 23 — first print post-Freight spin-off
MU (Micron) fiscal Q3 2026 earnings after close Wednesday June 24 — options pricing ~17% move
Fed bank stress test results released Wednesday June 24
May PCE Price Index (Fed's preferred inflation gauge) released Wednesday June 25 — last read was 3.8% YoY headline / 3.3% core
Q1 GDP final estimate also due June 25
Commerce Dept Section 232 copper tariff recommendation due to President by June 30
US-Iran peace talks: 60-day roadmap agreed, but substantive issues (nuclear program) excluded; Hormuz partially reopened

ideas.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
ideas.thesis: Micron reports fiscal Q3 2026 results on June 24 after the close, at what may be the most anticipated semiconductor print of the year. The company has structurally re-rated from cyclical memory maker to AI infrastructure play, with its entire 2026 HBM (high-bandwidth memory) capacity fully sold out, management guiding to a record ~81% gross margin in Q3 (up from 74.4% in Q2 and 36.8% a year ago), and consensus EPS implying ~$19.95 vs. $1.56 in the year-ago quarter. Last quarter's Q2 beat was 42% above the estimate ($12.20 vs. $8.60 expected), establishing a strong pattern of upside surprises. The options market is pricing roughly a 17% move in either direction — confirming this is a high-stakes, high-information event.
ideas.catalyst: Fiscal Q3 2026 earnings after close Wednesday June 24, 2026 — covering the March-May quarter; FY2027 guidance and HBM4 volume commentary are the key swing factors
ideas.edge: Consensus EPS ~$19.95 on $34.7B revenue; prior quarter beat by ~42% ($12.20 actual vs. $8.60 est.); analyst targets range from ~$400 (Goldman bear case) to $1,625 (UBS bull); MU closed at an all-time high of ~$1,134 on June 18; options-implied move of ~17% post-print implies a settlement range of ~$940-$1,327
ideas.bull_case: HBM4 demand from NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform is locked in, gross margins break 80%, management raises Q4 guidance above the ~$39.6B analyst estimate, and the stock re-rates further toward Cantor Fitzgerald's $1,500 target as the 'secular grower' thesis solidifies.
ideas.bear_case: The stock enters at an all-time high priced for perfection; Goldman Sachs maintains a ~$400 target arguing margins are a cyclical peak, not a floor; synchronized capex expansion by SK Hynix and Samsung could produce oversupply in 2027-2028, and any soft guidance or miss on HBM commentary could trigger the full ~17% downside implied by options.

ideas.name: FedEx Corporation
ideas.thesis: FedEx reports Q4 FY2026 earnings after the close Tuesday June 23 — the first quarterly print as a streamlined pure-play parcel company following the June 1 IPO spin-off of FedEx Freight. The stock has already outperformed (+40% YTD, +80% over 52 weeks), but Bernstein's David Vernon argues the market is still undervaluing the sum-of-parts: he estimates ~$55-60/share in excess cash plus ~$20/share from the retained 19.9% Freight stake, implying FDX trades at just ~10.7x standalone forward earnings vs. a broader industrials multiple. The company has beaten EPS estimates in each of the last four quarters, most recently by a wide margin (+27.7% surprise in Q2).
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings after close Tuesday June 23, 2026 — key watch items are FY2027 guidance (first full year ex-Freight), Network 2.0 cost savings progress (targeting $2B cumulative by 2027), and Amazon partnership volume commentary
ideas.edge: Bernstein price target $424, average analyst target ~$407-412 vs. ~$336 current price = ~25-30% implied upside; trades at ~10.7x forward earnings (Bernstein estimate); 17 of 21 analysts rate it Strong Buy; options pricing ~7.7% move at print; FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance raised to $19.30-$20.10 (from $17.80-$19.00)
ideas.bull_case: Network 2.0 savings ramp faster than expected, Amazon partnership volumes accelerate (aided by UPS ceding market share), FY2027 guidance comes in above the ~$22 consensus EPS, and the Freight stake is monetized at a premium — all of which would close the 25%+ gap to analyst targets.
ideas.bear_case: Morgan Stanley's Ravi Shanker trimmed his target to $160 citing margin headwinds, post-spin reporting opacity (full segment disclosure not until late October), and MD-11 fleet grounding costs (~$55M impact in Q4). The stock is already near 52-week highs so any soft guidance on FY2027 would punish it disproportionately.

ideas.name: Freeport-McMoRan Inc.
ideas.thesis: The Commerce Secretary must deliver a Section 232 copper tariff recommendation to President Trump by June 30 — a binary, dated policy catalyst now 8 days away. FCX is the largest US-listed copper producer and the most direct domestic equity beneficiary of tariff protection: domestic producers face no import duty while foreign competitors would face a proposed 15% starting January 2027 rising to 30% in 2028. LME copper is already near record highs (+10% YTD) and the COMEX-LME spread has widened to ~$400/t, reflecting pre-positioning. Jefferies specifically named FCX as a top beneficiary of a US tariff regime.
ideas.catalyst: Commerce Dept Section 232 copper tariff recommendation due to President Trump by June 30, 2026 — a hard statutory deadline; Jefferies named FCX as a direct beneficiary
ideas.edge: LME copper near record highs, up ~10% YTD; COMEX-LME spread at ~$400/t signals ongoing tariff pre-positioning; ING forecasts a ~35kt global copper deficit in 2026 (tightest in 15 years per Goldman); Morgan Stanley assigns a 43% probability to a phased tariff starting at 15% in Jan 2027. FCX generates meaningful leverage to copper price: every $0.10/lb move in copper price is estimated at ~$130-140M in annual EBITDA.
ideas.bull_case: Commerce Dept recommends a phased refined copper tariff; FCX's US-mined output faces no duty while Chilean/African imports do, improving relative economics; renewed stockpiling accelerates into year-end and copper price gaps higher.
ideas.bear_case: The outcome is binary and the July 2025 precedent (when a 50% tariff was feared but refined copper was carved out entirely) shows Trump can surprise to the downside; a delay or rejection would trigger rapid COMEX inventory unwind and narrow the US copper premium, pressuring FCX alongside the rest of the sector.
